examples.title: Peterson's Solution for Two Processes
examples.description: Software solution using shared variables for mutual exclusion
examples.explanation: Peterson's solution uses two shared variables: flag[2] to indicate desire to enter CS, and turn to break ties. Process sets flag true, sets turn to other, then waits if other wants CS and it's other's turn. Satisfies mutual exclusion, progress, and bounded waiting.
examples.timeComplexity: O(1) for entry/exit in best case, O(n) worst case due to waiting
examples.spaceComplexity: O(1) - only two shared variables needed

examples.title: Test-and-Set Lock Implementation
examples.description: Hardware atomic instruction for mutual exclusion using spinlock
examples.explanation: Test-and-set atomically reads the lock value and sets it to true in one indivisible operation. If it returns false (lock was free), the process enters CS. If returns true, it loops and tries again. This is a spinlock implementation.
examples.timeComplexity: O(1) for acquiring lock in best case, O(n) worst case with contention

examples.title: Semaphore Implementation for Mutual Exclusion
examples.description: Using semaphore for process synchronization and mutual exclusion
examples.explanation: Semaphore provides atomic operations. wait() decrements value and blocks if negative. signal() increments and wakes waiting process if any. For mutual exclusion, initialize to 1 so only one process can enter CS at a time.
examples.timeComplexity: O(1) for both wait and signal operations
examples.spaceComplexity: O(n) for waiting queue where n is max blocked processes

examples.title: Monitor Implementation Example
examples.description: High-level synchronization construct with automatic mutual exclusion
examples.explanation: Monitor ensures mutual exclusion automatically - no explicit lock needed. Processes call monitor procedures and are automatically synchronized. Condition variables allow processes to wait for specific conditions inside the monitor.
examples.timeComplexity: O(1) for procedure calls, depends on wait/signal
